name: Pillars Fund
mission: Pillars Fund amplifies the leadership, narratives, and talents of Muslims in the United States to advance opportunity and justice for all.
theoryOfChange: Tapping into Muslim storytelling to transform society and challenge stereotypes through authentic representation in film and media.
scoreSummary: Pillars Fund shows strong impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: Research published in partnership with Annenberg Inclusion Initiative and Ford Foundation

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 3.1% of revenue ($185,000 on $5,966,431)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue $5-50M, CEO pay above 2% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.evidence: Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fi_sabilillah (+5)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 25/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; moderate program ratio (≥65%)
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

judge_issues.message: Expenses ($4,783,807) exceed revenue ($2,398,957) by 99% - may be drawing down reserves

score_details.score_summary: Pillars Fund shows strong impact and moderate alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Pillars Fund is a grant-making organization and Muslim community foundation named in reference to the five pillars of Islam, the third of which is Zakat. Pillars is focused on funding secular non-profit programs supporting American Muslims in media, public relations, and leadership development. (Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pillars_Fund)

all_citations.claim: The organization maintains a 74.3% program expense ratio.
Pillars Fund reported total revenue of $5,966,431 and holds 20.7 months of working capital.
The mission is to strengthen relations with American Muslims by amplifying talents and supporting nonprofits.
The organization invests and leverages funds to support leadership and external affairs.
The organization manages storytelling grants and fellowships to support Muslim creators.
The organization produces multimedia publications to amplify Muslim voices.
The organization provides specific instructions for charitable giving and donations.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Pillars Fund earns a 62/100, reflecting a solid financial foundation and specialized cultural impact, though its niche focus and capacity-building model result in a medium alignment score for general donors.
areas_for_improvement: Limited third-party impact evaluations for long-term outcomes
Moderate alignment for donors seeking direct poverty alleviation

dimension_explanations.alignment: Pillars Fund offers a medium fit for donors, as its mission focuses on long-term cultural and religious urgency rather than immediate humanitarian aid [3][4]. While it provides a clear pathway for sadaqah-eligible giving, its specific focus on the arts and leadership may not align with all donor priorities [8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high effectiveness in its niche, maintaining a 74.3% program expense ratio and producing significant multimedia publications to shift public discourse [1][2][7]. Its capacity-building model is supported by a robust financial position, including total revenue of $5,966,431 and 20.7 months of working capital [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Pillars Fund empowers American Muslims by strategically investing in storytelling and leadership to reshape cultural narratives.
strengths: Strong financial stability with 20.7 months of working capital
High fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.12 to raise every $1
Focused program strategy targeting cultural and narrative change
baseline_narrative.summary: Pillars Fund is a non-profit organization dedicated to strengthening relations between American Muslims and the broader public by amplifying Muslim talents and leadership [3]. Through initiatives like the Pillars Artist Fellowship and Storytelling Grants, the organization provides critical resources to creators and nonprofit leaders to ensure authentic representation [4][5].

all_citations.quote: To strengthen relations with American Muslims and all Americans.

all_citations.claim: Pillars Artist Fellowship and Media Labs
all_citations.quote: Programs: Storytelling Grants, Pillars Artist Fellowship, Media Labs

all_citations.claim: 74.3% of its expenses toward program services
Bronze Seal of Transparency from Candid

all_citations.quote: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring

all_citations.claim: clearly state its policy on zakat

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Pillars Fund earns a 62/100, reflecting a solid financial foundation and specialized cultural impact, though its niche focus and capacity-building model result in a medium alignment score for general donors.

areas_for_improvement.detail: The charity currently holds a <cite id="17">Bronze Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, suggesting room for more detailed public reporting.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization has experienced a <cite id="5">revenue CAGR of -53.4%</cite> over the last three years, indicating a need for more sustainable growth strategies.

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB assessment data is not currently available for this organization.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's <cite id="6">20.7 months of working capital</cite> provides a significant safety net against these revenue fluctuations.
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="5">significant revenue decline</cite> and limited third-party impact evaluations for narrative-change work.

confidence.notes: Strong financial data but limited third-party impact evaluation.

alignment.explanation: Strong fit for donors interested in <cite id="1">American Muslim storytelling</cite> and cultural leadership, though it is <cite id="19">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE only</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The organization could <cite id="20">clearly state its policy on zakat</cite> to better guide faith-based donors.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust Pillars Fund due to its <cite id="11">9-member board</cite> and <cite id="12">88% board independence</cite>. While not fully rated by Charity Navigator, it maintains a <cite id="17">Bronze Seal from Candid</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity could <cite id="17">pursue a higher Candid seal</cite> by sharing more comprehensive outcome data.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="10">74.3% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="2">$30 million leveraged fund</cite> for Muslim leadership.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="18">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-effectiveness analysis.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Developing theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Supports nonprofit organizations that amplify American Muslim talents and leadership.

rich_narrative.headline: Pillars Fund empowers American Muslims by strategically investing in storytelling and leadership to reshape cultural narratives.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to invest in the long-term cultural influence of American Muslims through media, arts, and leadership development.
donor_motivations: Value authentic representation in media and arts
Want to support a strategic grantmaker with high program efficiency
Prefer funding systemic narrative change over direct relief
Are comfortable with a Sadaqah-only giving status
giving_considerations: Gifts support a grantmaking intermediary rather than direct service delivery
Narrative change impact is measured over decades, not months
Organization has a strong financial buffer but declining recent revenue
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill Zakat obligations or those looking for immediate, quantifiable poverty alleviation metrics.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcomes like <cite id="16">multimedia publications</cite> and grant distributions, but lacks independent longitudinal studies.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Pillars Fund creates impact by <cite id="1">investing in Muslim leaders and storytellers</cite> to shift cultural perceptions and build community power.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in narrative change helps prove that storytelling can lead to shifts in public policy and social inclusion.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite a <cite id="5">revenue decline</cite>, the organization has a <cite id="21">$5.0M funding gap</cite> to reach its strategic goals in the cultural space.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its focus on <cite id="4">storytelling and media fellowships</cite> rather than direct legal advocacy or social services.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="6">20.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing a robust buffer for long-term strategic initiatives.
Pillars Fund directs <cite id="10">74.3% of its expenses toward program services</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of funds reach the community.
The organization is led by an <cite id="11">independent board of 9 members</cite>, with <cite id="12">88% of board members</cite> meeting independence criteria.

strengths_deep_dive: Pillars Fund excels in fundraising efficiency, spending only <cite id="13">$0.31 to raise every $1</cite> in revenue. This lean operation is paired with a sophisticated grantmaking strategy that distributed <cite id="9">$6,560,000 in total grants</cite> to 111 recipients, including major awards to the <cite id="14">Inner City Muslim Action Network ($155,000)</cite> and <cite id="15">Detroit Revival Engaging Muslims ($145,000)</cite>. Their impact is further evidenced by their production of <cite id="16">multimedia publications</cite> and the successful leveraging of external funds to support the American Muslim ecosystem.
rich_narrative.summary: Pillars Fund is a strategic grantmaking organization dedicated to <cite id="1">strengthening relations with American Muslims</cite> and all Americans by investing in storytelling and leadership. The organization operates as a capacity builder, having <cite id="2">invested and leveraged over $30 million</cite> to amplify Muslim talents and reshape cultural narratives. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $2,398,957</cite>, with a significant focus on domestic grantmaking. Their primary initiatives include the <cite id="4">Pillars Artist Fellowship and Media Labs</cite>, which provide resources to creators to ensure authentic representation in the arts and media. Despite a <cite id="5">3-year revenue CAGR of -53.4%</cite>, the organization maintains exceptional financial stability, holding <cite id="6">20.7 months of working capital</cite> in reserve. This financial cushion allows them to sustain long-term programs like the <cite id="7">Storytelling Grants</cite> even during periods of revenue fluctuation. The fund serves as a critical bridge, supporting <cite id="8">111 domestic grant recipients</cite> with a total grant distribution of <cite id="9">$6,560,000</cite> in the most recent reporting period, focusing on community-based organizations and cultural creators.
